name: rad-session
description: **4.0 — 8-doc standardization: /init merges CLAUDE-FRAGMENT.md from rad-planner, /startup gap-checks strategic docs, /wrapup prompts for DECISIONS.md updates.** Three-phase Claude Code workflow lifecycle with cross-machine continuity, soft-coupled with rad-planner 3.0 via the RAD 8-doc standard (docs/file-conventions.md). /init bootstraps a project once (deterministic stack detection via Python scripts, CLAUDE.md scaffold with FRAGMENT-merge or auto-generated/placeholder @-imports for PRD/ARCH/ASSUMPTIONS/DECISIONS/PLAN, gitignore handling for session files, rad-* plugin recommendations, plus a per-project plugin-bloat audit that proposes enabledPlugins disables). /startup orients each session (Phase 0 fetches origin and prompts to pull when behind before reading any handoff file; Phase 1.5 gap-checks the 5 strategic docs and soft-warns when missing; cross-machine handoff detection via hostname; cached resource discovery; stale-handoff auto-refresh from git log). /wrapup captures each session (recency-bounded conversation tagging, structured HANDOFF.md with 'what NOT to do' field, mechanical session-log derivation from HANDOFF, Phase 3.5 prompts to append tagged decisions to DECISIONS.md with sequence-numbered multi-line entries — skipped in --quick and non-interactive, CLAUDE.md prune auto-skipped when unchanged, auto-commit + prompted push of session files). /add-resource registers tools any time. Three Python scripts (detect-stack.py, detect-resources.py, audit-plugin-bloat.py) make stack, MCP, and plugin-bloat detection deterministic. PreCompact hook prevents silent context loss. Single-writer rule: rad-session owns CLAUDE.md / HANDOFF.md / session-log; rad-planner owns the strategic and operational docs. Skills run in the session model — if you want Haiku speed on a short wrapup, run /model haiku explicitly before invoking the skill.
